Analyze the current state of the codebase against fix_plan.md to prioritize Batch 2 fixes.
Refactor insertFlowSpacerIfNeeded in slideVisualEditorIframe.js to prevent layout shifts in flex rows as per the requirements in fix_plan.md.
Update the vocabulary parsing regex in flashVocabParse.js to support digits and parentheses at the beginning of tokens, improving flexible validation.
Implement translation concurrency control and cancellation logic in flashVocabOpenAiTranslate.js to ensure state safety during high-frequency edits.
Refactor _extract_first_json_object in src/flash_translate_service.py to use a more robust parsing strategy that handles nested braces correctly.
Perform a consistency check on classifyFlashVocabLine and highlight logic in flashVocabParse.js to ensure all rejection states are handled robustly.
Finalize integrated testing for the flashcard bookmarking feature and verify state persistence across session switches.
Review recent UI tweaks in fix_ui.md and outline the next highest-impact improvements for the chatbot cards and message area.
Refine the full set starter cards so the copy, button labels, and actions stay consistent across quiz, slide, PDF, and THPTQG flows.
Investigate why the new slide experience occasionally loses the selected theme and patch the state handoff.
Plan the cleanup for duplicate slide_html_template directories and identify every backend or script path that must be updated.
Move the slide template source of truth fully under frontend/slide_html_template and remove redundant root references safely.
Audit slideShellSrcdoc scaling logic and fix the mismatch between preview size and the rendered slide frame.
Prevent icon badges in the slide shell from shifting when long headings or dynamic content are injected.
Fix the sealife slide template so decorative icons stay anchored after responsive scaling.
Update the slide shell to avoid redundant DOM work while recomputing layout on resize.
Review the slide editor selection overlay and explain why it jitters when the title block changes height.
Adjust slideVisualEditorIframe so title edits do not cause the toolbar handles to jump between frames.
Reduce layout shift in the visual editor when inline icons and text blocks are remeasured.
Stabilize the flow spacer insertion logic for flex rows inside the slide visual editor.
Verify the slide body application script handles renamed templates like 4.space-bright.html correctly.
Fix apply_slide_shell_bodies.py so missing slide templates fail loudly instead of silently skipping updates.
Add a safer lookup path for slide shell body generation after consolidating template directories.
Ensure the slide shell does not enter a resize loop when MutationObserver reacts to its own DOM writes.
Investigate the slide preview for infinite reflow conditions and guard against recursive layout updates.
Clean up slideExperienceView teardown so reopening the same deck does not leave stale listeners behind.
Trace a possible memory leak when closing full-set or slide experiences repeatedly and patch the cleanup path.
Add inline text editing support to the slide visual editor for standard heading and paragraph nodes.
Fix text hit-testing in slideVisualEditorIframe so clicks land on the intended editable node instead of the wrapper.
Review the new text editing flow and harden it against nested spans, icons, and decorative wrappers.
Allow text color changes in the slide editor without corrupting inline markup or selection ranges.
Correct the editor logic that fails to recognize certain text nodes after the first edit operation.
Improve slide editor performance by trimming repeated DOM scans during selection and toolbar refresh.
Add undo support for text and object edits inside the slide visual editor.
Extend the visual editor to insert uploaded images into valid drop zones inside each slide layout.
Implement delete-image controls in the slide editor and make sure keyboard undo restores the removed asset.
Fix the slide image picker state so multiple uploads in one session do not leak previous object URLs.
Refactor slideExperienceImagePicker to separate file validation, preview generation, and editor dispatch.
Audit the image insertion transport path and remove insecure assumptions around remote asset URLs.
Prepare a plan for improving the slide mobile preview so editing and navigation remain usable on small screens.
Fix mobile slide preview assertions in Playwright after the recent experience layout adjustments.
Refresh the THPTQG slide template styling so the title, subtitle, and callouts feel more balanced in presentation mode.
Expand the bright space slide template into a more polished long-form lesson layout with better spacing and hierarchy.
Build out the dark space slide template with complete content blocks instead of placeholder sections.
Redesign the sealife slide template to better support richer educational content and image distribution.
Rework the friendly slide template to support more complete lesson slides while preserving readability.
Push the comic slide template toward a full lesson-ready layout with stronger panel structure and captions.
Fix overflow edge cases in the comic slide template when speech bubbles or panel titles become long.
Adjust the comic slide title treatment so it stays visually aligned across desktop preview and presentation mode.
Continue polishing the comic slide layout to avoid panel collisions in medium-height content scenarios.
Review slideShellSrcdoc for the comic template and make preview rendering match the exported DOM more closely.
Remove redundant properties and hacks from the comic template after the latest layout pass.
Investigate why generated slide content sometimes loses visual balance after shell hydration and patch the source doc builder.
Update sessionContentPrep so slide content arrives in the shell with cleaner section grouping and fewer malformed nodes.
Improve slideExperienceView state transitions when users switch between overview, edit, and present modes repeatedly.
Add a PDF export API for slide decks that posts the current srcdoc and returns a downloadable file.
Create a Playwright-backed script to export slide srcdoc to PDF while preserving background graphics.
Make the PDF export pipeline wait for images, fonts, and animation frames instead of relying on a fixed delay.
Strip editor-only controls and contenteditable attributes before capturing slide decks as PDF.
Force each slide page to export at 1280x720 with clean page breaks and no extra margins.
Add frontend download handling for exported slide PDFs and ensure object URLs are revoked after use.
Write focused tests around triggerPdfDownload so repeated exports do not leave anchors or blobs behind.
Investigate browser channel fallback for PDF export and use Chromium when Edge is unavailable.
Review whether exported slide PDFs stay visually stable across multi-slide decks with mixed templates.
Add a full THPTQG entry point to the guided flow and wire it into the startup hub and chatbot actions.
Create the first THPTQG full-test experience view with catalog, test selection, and timed exam state.
Add mock full-test data for THPTQG and expose it through mockContentApi for local frontend development.
Persist THPTQG exam progress in the experience state service so users can resume after chat switches.
Make experience resume reopen THPTQG full-test sessions correctly from saved session history.
Ensure completed THPTQG attempts can return to chat without depending on browser history state.
Reopen saved THPTQG results automatically when the user selects a session from the sidebar.
Keep the THPTQG result overview tab active when question chips are clicked from the summary grid.
Build richer part-level navigation for THPTQG tests so users can jump between grouped question ranges cleanly.
Improve THPTQG review mode with clearer status labels for correct, wrong, skipped, and invalid items.
Style the THPTQG experience to feel native to the app instead of a bolted-on quiz screen.
Fix quiz CSS so long reading passages and grouped questions are easier to scan on both desktop and mobile.
Render bold markers from THPTQG reading passages so emphasis in the source bundle appears in the UI.
Extend quiz rich-text rendering to support underlined sentence markers for reading-comprehension prompts.
Repair THPTQG prompt generation so weak placeholders become useful instructions based on group context.
Handle long reading groups differently from short cloze groups in the THPTQG experience layout.
Split merged paragraph groups in the THPTQG mock bundle so paragraph-based questions reference the right blocks.
Normalize THPTQG bundle structure and remove empty context entries that create fake blank paragraphs.
Add a repair script for THPTQG paragraph groups and keep a baseline snapshot for safer iteration.
Investigate answer key mismatches introduced by OCR noise in imported THPTQG full tests.
Strengthen import_thptqg_fulltests.py so option parsing works for inline layouts without eating prompt initials.
Add tests for question-sequence parsing and answer leakage cleanup in the THPTQG import pipeline.
Strip leaked answer tables and explanation text from imported THPTQG question content.
Support lowercase and uppercase manual answer overrides in the THPTQG importer.
Sync the embedded THPTQG fallback bundle automatically after canonical JSON changes.
Add a normalization script that rewrites THPTQG mock data into a stable canonical format before syncing.
Review test 1 in the THPTQG bundle and fix reading/grouping issues that break the new experience view.
Fix test 2 through test 7 paragraph grouping and align cloze blanks and reading passages with their questions.
Build review notes for THPTQG tests 2 to 7 so recurring import defects can be repaired systematically.
Repair question prompts and passage alignment for THPTQG test 7, especially the long reading sections.
Investigate tests 8 to 10 for answer-key, paragraph, and context integrity issues, then patch the mock bundle.
Fix arrangement-style stems that render a dangling hyphen line before a-e labels in THPTQG views.
Add a one-off script for cleaning arrangement stems in mock 11 and sync the embedded bundle afterward.
Split the climate passage in mock 11 part 4 into real paragraphs so questions 31-40 map correctly.
Restore bold emphasis and paragraph structure for mock 11 reading sections around questions 21-30.
Improve the THPTQG view so grouped reading passages preserve paragraph spacing and highlighted text correctly.
Audit tests 10 to 15 for OCR artifacts, polluted options, wrong stems, and inconsistent passage wiring.
Build a batch repair script for THPTQG mocks 10 to 15 and use it to patch repeated data-quality defects.
Fix mock 12 passages, Udemy blanks, cultural diversity wording, and prompt quality based on the repair checklist.
Clean up tests 13 to 15 so passages, stems, and answer keys no longer contradict the reading content.
Adjust mock 15 questions 21-29 with better paragraph splits, bold emphasis, and underlined traffic-noise references.
Limit a problematic part in the later THPTQG mocks to the intended I-IV items instead of extra stray options.
Review thptqgFullTestExperienceView for result-summary edge cases after the latest mock-data fixes.
Improve result summary calculations so invalid questions do not distort part accuracy and score-on-10 output.
Add clearer chips or badges for flagged THPTQG questions during exam mode and review mode.
Verify that the chatbot can open a different THPTQG test from the catalog without jumping back to a previous result state.
Add or update Playwright coverage for reopening saved THPTQG results and switching among sessions.
Run a consistency pass on quizService rich-text helpers now that THPTQG stems use bold, underline, and arrangement splits.
Review recent mobile and chat smoke tests to make sure the expanded THPTQG flow did not regress basic navigation.
Tighten mockContentApi and quiz service integration so THPTQG content loads predictably in local and test environments.
Do a codebase-wide pass for stale slide_html_template root references after the frontend consolidation.
Cross-check Docker and API startup paths so slide PDF export and the THPTQG mock bundle both work in the same environment.
Update internal notes or checklists to reflect the recurring THPTQG repair patterns and the slide export constraints.
Make a prioritized plan for the next cleanup pass across slide editor stability, PDF export reliability, and THPTQG data quality.
Run a final review of the codebase state since April 21 and list any remaining high-risk regressions before demo use.
Finish with a targeted polish pass on the website so full THPTQG, balanced slides, and stable PDF download all feel production-ready.

Sửa resize trong frontend/js/chatbot/slide/slideVisualEditorIframe.js (hàm applyResize / onHandlePointerDown, xử lý handle e/w/n/s và góc nw/ne/sw/se, kèm placeCornerAnchored, lockTextBoxForEdgeResize, lockTextBoxForCornerResize).
Mục tiêu UX giống Canva: cạnh/nút kéo theo đúng hướng trực giác — kéo ra xa phía nút đang giữ thì phần tử to ra, kéo ngược về phía nút (vào trong) thì nhỏ lại, không bị “to ngược”.
Sửa lệch chuột: cạnh đang kéo phải bám sát vị trí con trỏ (tính delta theo điểm neo của handle hoặc tọa độ cạnh lúc pointerdown, không để tích lũy lệch do scale iframe/transform, data-edit-x/data-edit-y, hoặc do đổi width/font-size text không đồng bộ với rect).
Giảm độ nhạy / giật: giảm gain (ví dụ scale delta, smoothing, hoặc bỏ/bớt công thức kiểu (dx+dy)/2 / 1+d/200 nếu gây nhảy); text box ưu tiên thay đổi width theo pixel chuột trước, font/chiều cao chỉnh sau cho mượt.
Kiểm tra lại sau khi có slide-panel-fit / zoom shell.

Currently, it looks like the AI generation only continues while the user stays on the current chat session/page. If the user switches to another chat session, the generation should NOT stop.

In the React/Next.js app at C:\Users\giakh\Project\Project082\A20-App-082, I need to understand how AI content generation works and what happens to it when the user switches chat sessions.

Please find and read these specific areas:

1. `frontend/js/chatbot/services/aiContentApi.js` — how AI generation fetches content, what AbortController or cancellation logic exists
2. `frontend/js/chatbot/services/backgroundFetchStore.js` — how background fetches are stored and tracked
3. `frontend/js/chatbot/controllers/experienceController.js` — how experiences are mounted/unmounted and what happens on session switch
4. Any component files that mount experiences (slideExperienceView, quizExperienceView, flashExperienceView, fullSetMixedExperienceView) — specifically look for AbortController, cleanup, cancel, or unmount logic that stops fetches
5. `frontend/js/chatbot/dom/experienceLayerView.js` — any cleanup on hide
6. Any place where `AbortController`, `signal`, `abort()`, or fetch cancellation is used

Report: where exactly generation gets cancelled/stopped when the user navigates away, what the fetch/generation mechanism is (streaming? polling? single fetch?), and what would need to change to keep it running. Be very thorough and include file paths + line numbers.
